---
title: "Methodological and translational foundations of the FDEP-TP framework: PACE-mFPCA pipeline, PTP/IEP classification, and clinical implications across obesity, type 2 diabetes, and metabolic surgery"
subtitle: "Lancet Diabetes & Endocrinology — Article (Original Research)"
author:
  - name: "Héctor M. Virgen-Ayala"
    email: hectorvirgenmd@gmail.com
    orcid: 0009-0006-2081-2286
    affiliations:
      - "Department of Surgical Clinics, Universidad de Guadalajara, Guadalajara, Mexico"
      - "Department of General Surgery, Hospital General de Zona No. 14, Instituto Mexicano del Seguro Social, Guadalajara, Mexico"
date: "2026-05-09"
format:
  docx:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=2.2cm
    fontsize: 11pt
    mainfont: "Georgia"
    monofont: "Menlo"
    linkcolor: blue
    citecolor: blue
keep-tex: false
bibliography: refs_A3.bib
csl: ../lancet.csl
---

<!--
Article A3 — procedural / translational layer (L3) of the FDEP-TP trilogy.
This file is a SKELETON committed at the start of the A3 branch (Day 15 per
meta-prompt §10·2).

Companions:
- A1 (conceptual / physiological): branch `lancet-A1-conceptual`
- A2 (mathematical / statistical): branch `lancet-A2-mathematical`
  (tag `A2-submitted-2026-05-09`)

A3 contains the complete PTP / IEP classification machinery and the clinical
implications.  It is the most translational and clinically actionable of the
three articles.

Each TODO marks prose to be drafted on Days 15–21.
-->

# Summary

<!-- TODO Day 16 — 300-word structured Summary in Lancet style:
Background / Methods / Findings / Interpretation / Funding.
A3-specific anchor figures (in addition to anti-F8 shared block):
- Type V (enhanced–mixed with residual dysglycaemia) = 43·3 % RYGBP, 30·7 % SG (Bonferroni q=0·035)
- Type III (infra-physiological) = 40·0 % obesity+T2DM, 36·8 % Post-CR, 29·5 % T2DM
- Type I·I = 32·2 % non-obese without T2D + 23·1 % not-classifiable (single-analyte arms)
Emphasis: pipeline + decision algorithm + clinical implications. -->

# Research in context

**Evidence before this study.** <!-- TODO Day 17 — search terms covering
data-driven phenotyping (Ahlqvist 2018, Udler 2018, German Diabetes Study,
Tübingen prediabetes), bariatric mechanism reviews (Madsbad 2014, Lobato 2025
metabolic-surgery commission, Rubino 2025 clinical-obesity definition),
incretin-based pharmacotherapy (Frías 2021, Heise 2022, Rosenstock 2023). -->

**Added value of this study.** <!-- TODO Day 17 — first PTP/IEP classification
framework with explicit eight-rule precedence, three-suffix glycaemic context,
and a reproducible 16-script pipeline; bariatric-dominant Type V signature
applies to both RYGBP and SG (not RYGBP-exclusive); Type III dominance in
caloric restriction reframes "remission" as persistent infra-physiological
signalling. -->

**Implications of all the available evidence.** <!-- TODO Day 19 — clinical
algorithm: PTP/IEP-informed decision tree for selecting agonists (single,
dual, triple receptor) and indication for metabolic surgery; cautions:
ecological proof-of-concept, percentile thresholds awaiting prospective
individual-participant validation. -->

# Introduction

<!-- TODO Day 15 — open with the clinical translational gap (anti-F8):
heterogeneity of postprandial enteropancreatic responses across health,
obesity, T2DM, caloric restriction, and metabolic surgery is well documented
but operationally unclassified; existing taxonomies decompose static variables
and do not generate clinically actionable algorithms keyed to multi-hormonal
periprandial dynamics.  The present article closes this gap by formalising
the PTP/IEP classification hierarchy (Types I–V with .i/.ii subtypes and a/b/c
glycaemic suffixes) and operationalising it with a reproducible pipeline. -->

# Methods

## Study design and data sources

<!-- TODO Day 15 — short paragraph (anti-F8 shared with A1 and A2 with prose
envelope rewritten); cite OSF DOI 10.17605/OSF.IO/3CZRE; SHA-256 of master
table; pre-registration v10·0 frozen 2026-04-22. -->

## Periprandial sampling and analyte panel

<!-- TODO Day 15 — Methods §Periprandial sampling shared with A1/A2 with
envelope rewritten.  Emphasise that **eleven analyte forms** enter the
per-analyte PTP layer; **nine** enter the joint Happ–Greven operator; glucose
and insulin are PTP inputs but NOT joint-operator components. -->

## Analytic pipeline (16 R scripts)

<!-- TODO Day 16 — A3-PRINCIPAL section: detailed walk-through of the five-layer,
sixteen-script pipeline (DI2 of A3 will visualise this).
Layer 1 — ingest and harmonisation: 00_inventory.R, parse_master_table.R, 01_harmonize.R
Layer 2 — pseudo-IPD generation (within 01_harmonize.R)
Layer 3 — estimation: 02_fpca_pace.R, 03_mfpca_happgreven.R, 04b_srsf_registration.R
Layer 4 — classification: 05_classify_ptp.R, 05b_iep_perm.R
Layer 5 — inference, bootstrap, sensitivity, figures: 06_inference.R,
06_lme4_scores.R, 06b_mahalanobis.R, 07_bootstrap.R, 08_figures.R,
08b_lancet_figs.R, 09_sensitivity.R; A3-specific 10_A3_methodological_figs.R
on Day 16. -->

## PTP and IEP classification

### PTP per-analyte vocabulary (nine labels)

<!-- TODO Day 17 — full enunciation of the nine-label PTP vocabulary:
Preserved · Borderline Impaired · Impaired · Blunted · Borderline Altered ·
Altered · Recovered · Borderline Enhanced · Enhanced.  Discordant_High and
Discordant_Low flags blocking Type I/II assignment.  Glucose suffixes a/b/c. -->

### Eight-rule precedence hierarchy for Integrated Enteropancreatic Pattern Types

<!-- TODO Day 17 — full enunciation of rules R1–R7 plus the eight-rule
precedence hierarchy (1) any non-glucose Altered → Type IV·II; (2) Borderline
Altered → Type IV·I; (3a/3b) Enhanced with glucose=a/no-L → Type II·II,
otherwise → Type V·II; (4a/4b) Borderline Enhanced → Type II·I or V·I;
(5) Impaired/Blunted → Type III·II; (6) Borderline Impaired → Type III·I;
(7) all Preserved → Type I·I; (8) ≥1 Recovered remainder Preserved/Recovered →
Type I·II.  Glycaemic suffixes a/b/c only on Types III, IV, V. -->

### Reference-distribution standardisation (referenced to A2 Theorems S1.7–S1.8)

<!-- TODO Day 17 — short remit-back paragraph. -->

## Statistical inference (referenced to A2)

> Multivariate inference, Pillai trace and FANOVA permutation, Mahalanobis
> distances, simultaneous bootstrap bands, and the four pre-specified
> sensitivities (±0·5 SD threshold; leave-one-axis-out; ρ-AR(1); challenge-class
> pooling) are described in detail in companion paper A2 (Lancet Diabetes &
> Endocrinology, branch `lancet-A2-mathematical`, tag
> `A2-submitted-2026-05-09`).  F values are reported as cohort-separation
> magnitudes per Procedure L3·9 of A2.

## Software, reproducibility, and reporting

<!-- TODO Day 19 — boilerplate identical structure to A2 with prose envelope
rewritten.  STROBE checklist; TRIPOD+AI declared **pending** (extension of the
framework to clinical individual-participant prediction will be TRIPOD+AI when
the prospective validation is undertaken — A3 opens the line, does not close
it). -->

## Role of the funding source

There was no funding for this study. The author had full access to all data and final responsibility for the decision to submit for publication.

# Findings

## Cohort and panel composition

<!-- TODO Day 16 — Table 1 (anti-F8 shared with A1, A2). -->

## PTP per-analyte distribution

<!-- TODO Day 17 — across the 8,050 analyte–subject pairings:
Preserved 67·1%, Borderline Impaired 8·6%, Discordant_Basal 6·3%,
Borderline Altered 5·8%, Discordant_High 4·8%, Altered 3·7%, Impaired 2·2%,
Discordant_Low 1·5%, zero Blunted in this run.  Cohort patterns. -->

## Integrated Enteropancreatic Pattern Types I–V — three signatures

### Bariatric signature: Type V is bariatric-dominant, not RYGBP-exclusive

<!-- TODO Day 18 — Type V = 43·3% RYGBP and 30·7% SG (Bonferroni q=0·035).
Both retain markedly elevated PYY total amplitude (z_{ξ1} > 2 SD); RYGBP
shows GLP-1 active enhancement; SG shows ghrelin-total Blunted-equivalent
depression consistent with fundic resection. -->

### Disease signature: Type III dominates obesity+T2DM and caloric restriction

<!-- TODO Day 18 — Type III = 40·0% obesity+T2DM, 36·8% Post-CR, 29·5% T2DM.
Type III·c (with glucose dysglycaemia) is modal in obesity+T2DM. -->

### Reference signature: Type I·I dominant in non-obese without T2D

<!-- TODO Day 18 — Type I·I = 32·2% non-obese without T2D + 32·2% obesity.
The reference cohort also shows 23·1% not-classifiable — structural
consequence of single-analyte source arms (150 pseudo-subjects from arms
that measured only one hormone), not a failure of reference standardisation. -->

## Cross-cohort separability and bootstrap stability (referenced to A2)

<!-- TODO Day 19 — short qualitative paragraph; quantitative detail in A2. -->

# Discussion

## Principal clinical findings

<!-- TODO Day 19 — three-sentence reframing of bariatric-dominant Type V,
T2DM Type III dominance, and caloric-restriction Type III dominance. -->

## Comparison with existing taxonomies

<!-- TODO Day 19 — Ahlqvist 2018, Udler 2018, German Diabetes Study, Tübingen,
PREDICT (Berry 2020), Glucotypes (Hall 2018).  Static cross-sectional vs
multivariate-functional joint dynamics. -->

## Clinical implications: agonist selection and metabolic surgery indication

<!-- TODO Day 19 — A3-PRINCIPAL section: clinical-decision algorithm keyed
on PTP/IEP Types — single-receptor agonists (semaglutide), dual receptor
(tirzepatide), triple receptor (retatrutide, the "Tipo VI" extension); link to
2025 Lancet D&E commission on metabolic surgery (Lobato 2025; Rubino 2025).
DI4 will visualise the decision tree. -->

## Mechanistic interpretation (referenced to A1)

> The mechanistic interpretation of the four leading mFPC eigenfunctions —
> distal L-cell dominance (PC1), proximal-vs-distal sequencing (PC2), biphasic
> glucose–insulin coupling (PC3), and ghrelin tone (PC4) — is the subject of
> companion paper A1 (Lancet Diabetes & Endocrinology, branch
> `lancet-A1-conceptual`).

## Strengths and limitations

<!-- TODO Day 20 — clinical strengths; six declared limitations identical to
A1/A2 with envelope rewritten:
(i) ecological cohort-time-arm; (ii) MFPCA panel n=350; (iii) percentile
thresholds awaiting prospective validation; (iv) protocol heterogeneity;
(v) agnostic to circadian phase / CGM context; (vi) single-analyte source
arms drive 23·1 % not-classifiable in the reference cohort. -->

## Implications for research and practice

<!-- TODO Day 20 — open the line for prospective TRIPOD+AI validation;
Tipo VI extension for triple-receptor agonists; CGM-augmented framework. -->

## Conclusion

<!-- TODO Day 21 — 3-4 sentence clinical synthesis. -->

# Contributors

HMVA conceived the PTP/IEP classification framework, derived the eight-rule precedence hierarchy, implemented the analytic pipeline, performed the empirical evaluation, designed the clinical-decision algorithm, drafted the manuscript, and approved the final version.

# Declaration of interests

The author declares no competing interests, no industry support, and no funding for this work.

# Data sharing

All digitised periprandial trajectories, the harmonised cohort corpus (`master_table.csv`, SHA-256 `2829cd78018e411783671ec00f849647858bda552cfa4ec23ad505ba9704a117`), the 16-script analytic pipeline, the article-specific outputs, and the reproducibility scripts are deposited at Zenodo (concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19743544; article-specific version DOIs cited in each manuscript) and GitHub (`sv8wmxnbp8-hash/EPP10` v1·3 and subsequent branches) under CC-BY 4·0. The repository permits full reproduction of every numerical claim and follows FAIR data principles. Pre-registration is at the Open Science Framework (DOI 10.17605/OSF.IO/3CZRE; project at https://osf.io/tr469, frozen 2026-04-22).

# AI/LLM disclosure

Analytic pipeline design, R code implementation, and manuscript drafting were supported by Claude (Anthropic, Opus 4.7 / Sonnet 4.6, May 2026). All statistical outputs, pre-registration decisions, and scientific claims were validated by the author against the v10·0 master analysis plan (OSF DOI 10.17605/OSF.IO/3CZRE) and the FDEP-TP framework v2·0 reference document.

# Acknowledgments

<!-- TODO Day 21 — clinical acknowledgments. -->

---

# References

::: {#refs}
:::

---

# Display item captions

**DI1 — Periprandial trajectory acquisition protocol.** <!-- TODO Day 16 caption. -->

**DI2 — Computational pipeline (16 R scripts in 5 layers).** <!-- TODO Day 16 caption. -->

**DI3 — Integrated Enteropancreatic Pattern Types I–V prevalence by cohort.** <!-- TODO Day 17 caption. -->

**DI4 — Clinical-decision tree: PTP/IEP-informed agonist selection and metabolic-surgery indication.** <!-- TODO Day 17 caption. -->

---

# Tables

## Table 1 — Cohort, panel, and reference adequacy

<!-- TODO Day 16 — anti-F8 shared with A1 and A2. -->

## Table 2 — IEP Type I–V prevalence by cohort with permutation q-values

<!-- TODO Day 17 — A3-specific table.  Source: 03_outputs/lancet_run_2026-05-08/tables/iep_prevalence_perm.csv. -->

## Table 3 — PTP per-analyte distribution

<!-- TODO Day 18 — 8,050 analyte–subject classifications by label and analyte. -->

## Table 4 — PTP/IEP-informed clinical algorithm

<!-- TODO Day 19 — agonist selection and metabolic-surgery indication keyed on
Type and glycaemic suffix.  Pre-specified caveat: hypothesis-generating, not
prediction. -->

# Supplementary material

## Supplementary S1 — Intensional definitions of Patterns I·I–V·II

<!-- TODO Day 18 — full Supplementary S1 with intensional definitions of every
sub-type from the eight-rule precedence and the three-suffix glycaemic
context. -->

## Supplementary S2 — Operational protocol (PRIMARY appendix)

<!-- TODO Day 19 — full acquisition protocol: meal composition, sampling
density, analyte preservation, immunoassay specifications, harmonisation rules.
This appendix is A3-DISTINCTIVE and supports the translational use of the
framework. -->
